articles.name: Why does the data in my report change over time?
articles.title: Why does the data in my report change over time?
articles.body:
Question

Why did my data change for dates in the past?

Each time I look at a report for a time period in the past, the data changes. For example, for the period of last month, the report showed a first reply time of 10 hours. A week later, the same report showed a first reply time of 9.7 hours. Why is this?

Answer

There are many reasons why past data may change over time:

  • If the data is sorted by the date refers to a solved ticket. When an older ticket finally gets solved, replied to, or given a satisfaction rating, that data will show up on past dates in the report. In addition, if you use the Solved tickets metric, as soon as one of those tickets switches back to pending or open, the ticket will no longer show in the report.
  • Data can also change when a solved ticket is re-opened. This impacts the number of solved tickets or full resolution time. For example, if a ticket is reopened and resolved during the time you last saw the report, the report will reflect the new time for solving that ticket.

  • Additionally, on a ticket, the first reply time target of SLAs does not start until the ticket gets a first public comment from an end user. Your first reply time average can change for this reason. For more information, see this article: Zendesk SLAs and first reply time.

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    Why does past data change over time in Explore reports? Why did my analytics or reporting metrics change for past dates? Why does data for last month change in my report?

    Past data changes over time when you sort the report by the date that refers to a solved ticket, or when an older ticket receives a solution, a reply, or a CSAT or customer rating. Data also changes when a ticket switches from a solved state back to a pending or open ticket status, or when an agent reopens and resolves a ticket during the time you last saw the report. Additionally, the first reply time target of an SLA or deadline does not start until the ticket receives a first public comment from an end user or customer, which changes your first reply time average.

articles.name: Troubleshooting issues with your live chat connection
articles.title: Troubleshooting issues with your live chat connection
articles.body:
This troubleshooting guide includes steps to troubleshoot and resolve common chat connection errors. Use this guide if you're experiencing one or more of the following issues:

  • A gray X appear in my chat window with multiple different visitors
  • A disconnect error message, such as the following:
    • Disconnected, attempting to reconnect
    • Disconnected. Please refresh dashboard
  • Delays in the Chat dashboard functionality or the dashboard is unresponsive
  • Messages are duplicated due to disconnects
  • Chats are regularly unassigned even when you have chat routing configured
Note: Zendesk Chat is designed to automatically reconnect after a disconnection. Reconnections typically take a few seconds.

Follow the steps below to resolve issues with your live chat connection:

Step 1: Determine if there is an issue with your web browser

  1. Clear your cache and cookies. Clearing your browsing data can resolve formatting or loading issues.
  2. Log into your account in a private browsing window, incognito window, and different web browsers. If you have success in a different web browser or private browsing window, your issue is most likely due to a browser extension or setting.
  3. Remove extensions or restore default browser settings that may block your ability to chat with customers.
  4. Restart your web browser or computer. Issues with Chat can be caused by a local-storage issue, low browser memory, or an outdated browser.

For more information, see this article: Zendesk Support system requirements.

Step 2: Check for connection blockers

Security restrictions or network settings can sometimes cause trouble with your connection. Reach out to an admin or IT person to determine if any of the following restrictions apply to your account:

  1. Switch to a different network to see if you experience the same issue. Testing from a different network is the simplest way to identify if there is an issue with your local network.
  2. Ask your team if a VPN is being used. If so, do you experience your issue off of the VPN?
  3. Do you have a firewall or any anti-virus software blocking traffic to your site or ability to chat? If so, does the issue still occur when you temporarily disable these features? Read our recommendations for configuring your firewall for use with Zendesk and public IP addresses.

For more information, see the article: How to configure your network for Zendesk Chat

Step 3: Check for Visitor issues

If you are having trouble with specific visitors disconnecting with your agents, check for the following issues.

The web browser of visitors

An issue with the browser of a visitor can result in a visitor disappearing from the visitor list. Determine if the agent is experiencing chat issues with multiple visitors, or with a specific visitor, IP address, or company. Often this issue can be resolved by the end user. Ask the visitor to perform similar troubleshooting steps detailed in step one as their web browser or mobile device is most likely the source of the issue. For more information, see the article: Zendesk Chat system requirements.

A high volume of visitors

High traffic of visitors can be the cause of disconnects between visitors and agents. The high-load dashboard should be enabled to see if this feature alleviates the performance issues. For more information, see the article: Browsing your site's visitors for live chat.

Step 4: Check your chat connection settings

Review your chat connection settings to determine the issue.

View the following metrics in your chat connection settings:

  • Server: The Chat dashboard should connect to a server that is geographically closest to you.
  • Connection Type: If this metric displays another value other than "Ws" (Websocket) you could have a firewall blocking your connection.
  • Status: You should see the option “connected.”
  • Connection Quality: An indicator of the connection quality between your network and Zendesk Chat servers. The more filled in with black the line is, the better your connection. If the filled-in portion of the line is less than 50%, test your connection.
  • Connection Progress: The number "100" indicates that you are successfully connected and any value less than "100" indicates a connection issue.
  • Uptime: The amount of time (seconds/milliseconds) detected from your browser that Chat is connected over the total time of the session. If you were recently disconnected or have had breaks in the connection, the first number (time connected) will be lower than the second number (total session length).
  • D/C Count: If the disconnection count (D/C Count) is higher than the Connected Count then you are most likely experiencing an issue with your Chat dashboard connection.
  • Server Timeouts: Indicates the number of disconnections on the server end.

The settings previously mentioned help determine the possible source of your chat connection issues. Report these issues to an account admin or an internal IT Team to further troubleshoot once you have narrowed down the source of the issue.

Note: If necessary, you can reset your connection by clicking on the Force reconnect button. Force reconnecting will disconnect any active chats.

Step 5: Contact Zendesk Customer Support

If you still experience issues after you complete the steps in this guide, contact Zendesk Customer Support and provide the following information:

  • When did this issue first occur? How many times has it happened?
  • How many agents are impacted? Which agents and visitors are involved?
  • Determine if all of the agents on the same network (WiFi) or the same location (IP addresses).
  • Provide any relevant screenshots of the issue.

  • Send a screenshot of your Chat Connection settings, Chat Dashboard diagnostics, and support details immediately after the issue occurs.

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    Why does a gray 'x' appear in my chat window? Why do I see a disconnect error message? Why is my inbox or Agent Workspace dashboard unresponsive? Why do chats regularly unassign?

    These issues indicate common live chat connection problems. Zendesk Chat automatically reconnects after a disconnection, which typically takes a few seconds. You can troubleshoot these issues by checking your web browser, network connection blockers, specific visitor browser issues, visitor traffic volume, and your chat connection settings.

    How do I clear browser issues blocking my chat? How do I fix an unresponsive inbox or Agent Workspace dashboard?

    You can determine if your web browser or inbox causes the connection issue. Follow these steps to resolve it:

    1. Clear your cache and cookies to resolve formatting or loading issues.
    2. Log into your account or instance in a private browsing window, incognito window, and different web browsers. Success in a different browser indicates an issue with a browser extension or setting.
    3. Remove extensions or restore default browser settings that block your ability to chat with customers or end users.
    4. Restart your web browser or computer to clear local-storage issues, low browser memory, or an outdated browser.

    How do I check for network connection blockers? Can a VPN block my chat connection? Can a firewall cause chat disconnects?

    Security restrictions or network settings cause trouble with your connection. Reach out to an admin, internal IT team, or IT person to check these restrictions:

    1. Switch to a different network to identify if there is an issue with your local network.
    2. Ask your team if a VPN is being used and test if the issue occurs off of the VPN.
    3. Determine if a firewall or anti-virus software blocks traffic to your site or your ability to chat by temporarily disabling these features.

    Why do specific visitors or customers keep disconnecting? Why do customers disappear from my visitor list?

    An issue with the web browser of a specific visitor, customer, or end user can cause them to disappear from the visitor list. Determine if the agent or assignee experiences issues with multiple visitors or with a specific visitor, IP address, or company or organization. Ask the visitor to clear their cache and cookies, use a private browsing window, remove extensions, or restart their web browser or mobile device.

    Can high traffic cause chat disconnects? How do I fix chat drops during high volume?

    High traffic or a high volume of visitors causes disconnects between visitors and agents or assignees. You can enable the high-load dashboard to see if this feature alleviates the performance issues.

    How do I check my chat connection settings? What does the connection quality line mean in chat diagnostics? What does Ws mean in chat connection type?

    Review the metrics in your chat connection settings to determine the source of the issue:

    • Server: The inbox or Agent Workspace dashboard must connect to a server geographically closest to you.
    • Connection Type: If this metric displays a value other than "Ws" (Websocket), a firewall might block your connection.
    • Status: You must see the option "connected."
    • Connection Quality: This line indicates the connection quality between your network and Zendesk Chat servers. Test your connection if the black filled-in portion of the line is less than 50%.
    • Connection Progress: The number "100" indicates a successful connection, while any value less than "100" indicates a connection issue.
    • Uptime: This metric tracks the time your browser connects over the total session length. Disconnections or breaks lower the first number.
    • D/C Count: If the disconnection count is higher than the Connected Count, you experience an issue with your dashboard connection.
    • Server Timeouts: This metric indicates the number of disconnections on the server end.

    Can I reset my chat connection? What happens if I click Force reconnect?

    Yes, you can reset your connection by clicking on the Force reconnect button. Clicking this button disconnects any active chats.

    How do I contact Zendesk Customer Support for chat connection issues? What information should I provide to help desk support for chat disconnects?

    If you complete the troubleshooting steps and still experience issues, contact Zendesk Customer Support. Provide the following details:

    1. The time the issue first occurred and its frequency.
    2. The number of impacted agents or assignees, and the specific agents and visitors involved.
    3. Whether all impacted agents are on the same network or location.
    4. Relevant screenshots of the issue, including a screenshot of your Chat Connection settings, Chat Dashboard diagnostics, and support details taken immediately after the issue occurs.

articles.name: What is the difference between the Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets?
articles.title: What is the difference between the Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets?
articles.body:
Question

What is the difference between these different Support datasets in Explore? In what situation is it better to use one dataset over the other?

Answer

While the Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets all allow you to report on ticket data, they approach that reporting from different perspectives.

  • The Tickets dataset provides data on current ticket information
  • The Updates history dataset provides data about actions taken on tickets throughout their lifetime
  • The Backlog dataset provides a snapshot of your ticket data at a specific time in the past. To learn more about this dataset, see Analyzing your ticket backlog history with Explore.

Find an example of how the different datasets work in this article: What is the difference between the solved tickets and tickets solved metrics in Explore?

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is the difference between Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets? What is the difference between Support datasets in Explore or reporting or analytics? In what situation is it better to use one dataset over the other?

The Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets all allow you to report on ticket data, but they approach that reporting from different perspectives. The Tickets dataset provides data on current ticket information. The Updates history dataset provides data about actions taken on tickets throughout their lifetime. The Backlog dataset provides a snapshot of your ticket data at a specific time in the past.

articles.name: When do chats time out?
articles.title: When do chats time out?
articles.body:
Question

What is the expected behavior for chat sessions to time out? When do idle conversations time out? Why can't I end a chat in messaging?

Answer

Live chat and messaging are different experiences in Zendesk. Live chat uses a real-time chat session that can end or time out, for example when a visitor leaves the website. Messaging is asynchronous and does not use a customer-facing session that the end user can end.

A conversation ends differently based on whether your account uses live chat or messaging. Click the tiles below to learn how to end chat or messaging conversations in different setups.

End a live chat session as an end user

In live chat, the visitor controls the chat session. The session remains active until the visitor ends it in one of these ways:

  • The visitor closes their browser window and the conversation times out
  • The visitor minimizes their chat with an agent and the conversation times out
  • The visitor remains idle on the website and the conversation times out

  • The visitor ends the chat by clicking the options menu icon and selecting End chat

In Chat Mobile SDKs, the chat conversation ends after any of these conditions:

  • No activity on the chat for one hour
  • The visitor is disconnected for one hour
  • The agent ends the chat, which resets the session disconnect timeout to five minutes
  • The visitor ends the chat, which immediately terminates the session
  • If push notifications are disabled, the session times out 30 minutes after the user backgrounds the app

For idle status details, see these notes:

  • If the visitor closes the browser with no windows open that contain the chat widget, the session times out within 20 seconds to two minutes
  • If a visitor using a mobile browser minimizes, switches tabs, or closes the browser containing the chat, the session times out within 20 seconds to two minutes
  • If the visitor idles on a website with the widget, the session stays open for 20 minutes before it ends automatically. An idle status means no mouse or keyboard input on the website for 10 minutes. The user does not need to interact with the widget, but must be actively working on the page where the widget loads. A variance of up to five minutes can occur depending on their connection to Chat.

Leave a live chat session as an agent

An agent can leave a live chat, but not end the session. The widget remains open for the end user until the end user ends the session. However, an agent can leave the chat without the session ending.

To leave a live chat session:

  1. Click the X button in the chat window

  2. Click End chat in the confirmation dialog

If an agent leaves a chat session, the end user can reopen the chat. In this situation, the agent will experience the following:

  • The chat returns to the agent's queue after an end user sends another chat message
  • The previous chat history is present for the agent because it is still the same chat conversation
  • Any subsequent messages are added to the chat transcript
  • After the live chat ends, all additional messages are added to the chat transcript history as a single entry
Remember: If an agent never joined the chat, that is a missed chat, and the conversation is moved into a ticket. A missed chat ticket is not created until the visitor ends the session.

For more information, see Why can my customers still chat after all agents have gone offline?

End a chat session in Agent Workspace

In Agent Workspace, end users can still end chats in all the ways listed above. The only difference in Agent Workspace is that the agent can end a chat in Agent Workspace with an end user with these steps:

  1. In Agent Workspace, click End chat

  2. In the confirmation dialog, click End chat

  3. The chat shows the visitor that the agent left the chat session

End a conversation in messaging

Unlike live chat, in messaging a session is not a customer-facing concept. The visitor cannot end a session. Instead, conversations move between active and inactive states.

  • A messaging conversation is Active when the end user has sent a message recently
  • A messaging conversation becomes Inactive after a specified time with no activity by the end user, or when the ticket status (or status category) changes to On-hold, Pending, or Solved. By default, Zendesk defines a message as inactive after 10 minutes with no response from the end user. Admins can modify the inactivity period.

The ticket's activity state, as well as the ticket status, affect routing, business rules, and agent capacity. For example, if you use omnichannel routing and have not turned on messaging activity routing, turn on automatic release capacity and set your inactivity period for messaging to help agents manage capacity more efficiently.

Messaging uses user presence to identify if end users are still on the website or have abandoned the conversation by closing the website. User presence helps prioritize active conversations over abandoned ones and increases agent utilization by releasing capacity when the end user abandons the conversation.

Messaging conversations can also end when an agent’s capacity is released. This closes the session automatically and prevents the end user from reopening it. The terminal ticket status can be set to On-hold, Pending, or left unchanged for flexible workflows.

Note: Omnichannel routing is enabled as the default routing experience for Zendesk Suite accounts created after December 5, 2024.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is live chat or messaging? How do live chat and messaging differ? What is the expected behavior for chat sessions to time out?

Live chat uses a real-time support conversation session that ends or times out when a customer or website visitor leaves your brand website. Messaging is asynchronous and does not use a customer-facing session that the customer or end user can end; instead, conversations move between active and inactive states based on customer activity or ticket progress status.

How do I end a live chat session as an end user? How can a customer or visitor close a real-time chat?

You can end a active live chat session as an end user or customer in several ways: close your browser window containing the chat widget, minimize the chat with an agent, remain idle on the website, or click the options menu icon and select End chat.

Can I end a chat in messaging? Why can't I close a messaging conversation?

No. In messaging, a session is not a customer-facing concept. The customer or visitor cannot end a session because messaging conversations move automatically between active and inactive states.

How do I leave a live chat session as an agent? How can a team member leave a real-time chat?

You can leave a live chat session as an agent or support person by following these steps:

  1. Click the X button in the chat window.
  2. Click End chat in the confirmation dialog box.
The chat session remains open for the end user, and it returns to your queue if the customer sends another message.

 

How do I end a chat session in Agent Workspace? How can an agent or team member close a chat in the dashboard inbox?

You can end a live chat session completely as an agent within the Agent Workspace dashboard by following these steps:

  1. In the Agent Workspace inbox, click End chat.
  2. In the confirmation dialog box, click End chat.
The conversation then shows the visitor or customer that the team member left the chat session.

 

What are the session timeouts for Chat Mobile SDKs? When does a mobile app chat session expire?

A mobile SDK support conversation terminates immediately if the customer ends the chat, or it times out five minutes after an agent ends the chat. The session also closes automatically after one hour of no activity or one hour of customer disconnection. If you disable push notifications, the session times out 30 minutes after the user backgrounds the app.

What are the idle status timeout limits for web browser live chat? When do inactive web visitor chats close?

If a visitor closes their browser window or minimizes/switches tabs on a mobile browser, the session times out within 20 seconds to two minutes. If a visitor idles on a website with the widget, the session stays open for 20 minutes. Idle status means your customer provides no mouse or keyboard input on the web page for 10 minutes, with an allowed connection variance of up to five minutes.

When does a messaging conversation become inactive? What is the default messaging inactivity period?

By default, Zendesk defines a messaging conversation as inactive after 10 minutes with no response or activity from the end user. It also becomes inactive when the ticket progress status category changes to On-hold, Pending, or Solved. Admins can modify this inactivity period to help manage agent capacity.

articles.name: How do I verify that agents who receive “Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you” have the correct permissions?
articles.title: How do I verify that agents who receive “Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you” have the correct permissions?
articles.body:
Question

How can I ensure my agents have the correct permissions in live chat? Why am I getting the message Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you when launching the Chat dashboard? How can I sign in?

Answer

This message appears when Chat is disabled for the agent's profile. An admin can manage which agents can access Chat through the Admin Center. Contact your team if you are not an admin.

To enable Chat on your own profile

Note: When the only Chat seat is disabled, another admin from Support needs to enable Chat in your profile. If no other admin or license exists, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

To enable Chat for another agent

  1. In Admin Center, click People in the sidebar, then select Team > Team members
  2. From the list, click the agent you want to update
  3. On the Roles and access tab of the agent’s page, click Enabled next to Chat
  4. Update the user’s role to Agent or Admin as needed

  5. Click Save at the bottom of the page

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    Why am I getting the message "Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you" when launching the Chat dashboard? How can I sign in? How do I verify that agents who receive "Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you" have the correct permissions or access level? How can I ensure my agents or my team have the correct permissions in live chat?

    This message appears when Chat is disabled for the agent profile or support person profile. An admin manages which agents or our users can access Chat through the Admin Center. Contact your team or my team if you are not an admin.

    How do I enable Chat on my own profile? How can I turn on live chat permissions for my account?

    To enable Chat on your own profile, use these steps:

    1. From the Support dashboard or help desk inbox, click your Avatar in the top-right corner and select View profile.
    2. In the left panel, next to Role, click Manage in Admin Center.
    3. Click the checkbox under Access for Chat. Select Agent or Admin as needed.
    4. Click Save.

    How do I enable Chat for another agent or support person? How can I grant live chat access level to my team member?

    To enable Chat for another agent or team member, follow these steps:

    1. In Admin Center, click People in the sidebar, then select Team Team members.
    2. From the list, click the agent or our user you want to update.
    3. On the Roles and access tab of the agent's page, click Enabled next to Chat.
    4. Update the user's role to Agent or Admin as needed.
    5. Click Save at the bottom of the page.

    Can I enable Chat on my profile if the only Chat seat or subscription is disabled? What happens if no other admin or license exists?

    When the only Chat seat or subscription is disabled, another admin from Support or help desk needs to enable Chat in your profile. If no other admin or license exists, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: How do I export my data and reports from all Zendesk products?
articles.title: How do I export my data and reports from all Zendesk products?
articles.body:
Answer

Exports are available for each product. Select an option from the list to learn how to export data from the product you use.

Understand export permissions

Export capabilities vary by role and product.

Support tickets, users, and organizations

Guide knowledge base

Note: The Guide knowledge base has no CSV export option. You must use the help center API.

Messaging and legacy live chat

How to check your CSV export permission

To check your CSV export permission:

  • Go to Admin Center People Team Team members
  • Find your name and click to open your profile
  • Look at the Role field
  • If you have a custom role, click the role name to see your permissions
  • Look for CSV Export in the permissions list

To do a quick test: Go to a view with tickets and look for the Export button at the top-right. If it is present, you have the CSV Export permission. If it is missing, you do not have it.

Export data from Support

To export data directly from Support, see Export ticket, user, or organization data from your account.

Verify your plan against these requirements. If your plan allows exports, yet you don't see the Reports option, Contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help.

For more information, see:

  • How to export a list of users
  • What is the recommended method to regularly export Zendesk Support data?
  • What are my ticket export options?

  • Where is the data export option?

    Note: The ability to export data to a JSON, CSV, or XML file is available to admins and agents with a custom role that has export permissions.

Export reports from Analytics

To export a dashboard, see Export a dashboard tab.

Note: You can only export one tab at a time.

To export a report, see Work with reports from within a report.

Export data from Sales

To export data from Sales, see Export data from Zendesk Sales.

Export data from Knowledge

Important: There is no CSV export button for Knowledge content.

Available export methods

Method 1: Help center API article-by-article

This is best for complete data backups and migrations to another platform.

To export these items:

  • Article content for all locales
  • Article metadata such as authors, dates, and votes
  • Categories and sections structure
  • Article attachments that you must download separately

Limitations include the following:

  • Requires API knowledge or scripts
  • Must export articles through pagination
  • Time-consuming for large knowledge bases of more than 100 articles

For full documentation, see the help center API.

Method 2: Web scraping is not recommended

Web scraping uses tools to copy published articles from your public help center.

This is not recommended because of the following reasons:

  • Only gets published content and loses drafts and internal articles
  • Loses metadata such as author, creation date, votes, and comments
  • Breaks formatting often
  • Doesn't get article relationships or structure
  • Against the Zendesk Terms of Service if automated at scale

Method 3: Professional services

This is best for large-scale migrations and complex data transformations.

Zendesk professional services provide the following:

  • Complete knowledge base export
  • Preserves all metadata and relationships
  • Migration to another Zendesk instance or external platform
  • Data transformation and cleanup

Note: This is a paid service, so contact your account representative for a consultation.

Method 4: Community scripts

Community scripts are community-contributed export scripts found on the Zendesk Developer Community or GitHub.

Use this method with caution for the following reasons:

  • Community tools are not officially supported by Zendesk
  • May break if the API changes
  • Test thoroughly and review the code for security before you run the script

Quick comparison

Recommendation: For serious backup and migration needs, use the help center API or professional services.

Export data from Messaging or legacy live chat

Important: First, determine which product you have.

How to check if you use messaging or legacy live chat

  • Go to Admin Center > Channels. If you see Messaging and social, you have messaging. If you only see Chat, you have legacy live chat.
  • In messaging, conversations persist across sessions. In legacy live chat, sessions end when the customer closes the window.
  • Messaging is available on Suite plans such as Professional and Enterprise. Legacy live chat is a legacy product.

Messaging and Sunshine Conversations export

To export the data:

  • Go to Admin Center Channels Messaging and social Messaging
  • Click your messaging channel
  • Go to the Analytics or Export section to export conversation data. Location varies by setup.

You get conversation transcripts, metadata such as customer information, timestamps, and agent assigned, and message content. Alternatively, use the Sunshine Conversations API for a programmatic export.

Legacy live chat export

You can export your past chats from the History page in the legacy live chat dashboard.

To export the data:

  • Go to Admin Center Channels Chat Dashboard
  • Click Analytics
  • Export chat data as a CSV or scheduled email reports

Limitations include historical data retention that varies by plan and might not include very old chats. Alternatively, use the live chat API for a programmatic export.

For information on how to export conversion data, see Export Chat Conversion Tracking data.

Additional export considerations

Customer list add-on

To export end-user lists, some accounts have the customer list add-on which provides the following:

  • Enhanced user export capabilities
  • Scheduled user exports
  • User segment exports

This is not available on all plans. Contact your account representative to check eligibility and pricing.

To check if you have it: Go to Admin Center People Customers. Look for enhanced export options. If you only see a basic user export, you do not have the add-on.

Export frequency limits

Be aware of rate limits when you export data:

  • UI exports and CSV downloads: There is no strict limit on frequency, but very large exports of more than 100,000 records might time out. Split large exports by date range if needed.
  • API exports: Rate limits apply. Incremental exports are recommended for large datasets.

  • Scheduled exports: You can schedule daily and weekly exports for some data types to be emailed to specified addresses. This is automatic and does not count against manual export usage.

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    How do I check my CSV export permission? Can I check my data download rights? How do I find if I can export data?

    Scope and limitations

    To check your permissions, navigate to Admin Center People Team Team members. Select your name to open your profile and check the Role field. For custom roles, select the role name to view your permissions list and look for CSV Export. Alternatively, view a ticket list and look for the Export button at the top-right.

    Can I export Guide knowledge base articles to CSV? Can I download my help articles as a CSV? Is CSV export available for help center content?

    Scope and limitations

    No. The Guide knowledge base or help articles platform has no CSV export option. You must use the help center API to download this data.

    How do I export my Guide knowledge base? What is the best method to backup help articles? How can I migrate my help center content?

    Concept

    You can export your Guide knowledge base using the help center API article-by-article. This method serves best for complete data backups and platform migrations. It extracts article content for all locales, metadata like authors, dates, and votes, categories and sections structure, and article attachments which you download separately.

    Scope and limitations

    The help center API method requires API knowledge or scripts. You must export articles through pagination, which becomes time-consuming for large knowledge bases of more than 100 articles.

    Can I use web scraping to export help articles? Is web scraping recommended for Guide backups?

    Pro tip

    Web scraping is not recommended to copy published articles from your public help center. It loses drafts, internal articles, and metadata like author, creation date, votes, and comments. It often breaks formatting, fails to capture article relationships or structure, and violates the Zendesk Terms of Service if automated at scale.

    What do Zendesk professional services provide for data migration? Can professional services export my knowledge base?

    Concept

    Zendesk professional services provide complete knowledge base exports while preserving all metadata and relationships. They handle data transformation, cleanup, and migration to another Zendesk instance or external platform. This paid service works best for large-scale migrations and complex data transformations.

    Can I use community scripts to export my help center? Are community backup tools safe to use?

    Pro tip

    Use community-contributed export scripts from the Zendesk Developer Community or GitHub with caution. Zendesk does not officially support these tools, and they may break if the API changes. Test them thoroughly and review the code for security before you run the script.

    How do I check if I use messaging or legacy live chat? Am I using messaging or legacy chat? How can I verify my chat product type?

    Scope and limitations

    Navigate to Admin Center Channels. If you see Messaging and social, you use messaging. If you only see Chat, you use legacy live chat. In messaging, conversations persist across sessions, and the feature comes with Suite plans like Professional and Enterprise. In legacy live chat, sessions end when the customer closes the window.

    How do I export data from messaging or Sunshine Conversations? How do I download chat transcripts and conversation history?

    Scope and limitations

    Navigate to Admin Center Channels Messaging and social Messaging and select your messaging channel. Go to the Analytics or Export section to export conversation data. This download includes conversation transcripts, metadata like customer information, timestamps, assigned agents, and message content. Alternatively, use the Sunshine Conversations API for programmatic exports.

    How do I export data from legacy live chat? How do I download history from legacy chat?

    Scope and limitations

    Export past chats from the History page in the legacy live chat dashboard. Navigate to Admin Center Channels Chat Dashboard, select Analytics, and export chat data as a CSV or scheduled email reports. Historical data retention varies by plan and might exclude very old chats. Alternatively, use the live chat API for a programmatic export.

    What is the customer list add-on? Can I schedule user list exports? What are enhanced user export capabilities?

    Concept

    The customer list add-on or paid plugin provides enhanced user export capabilities, scheduled user exports, and user segment exports to download end-user lists. This feature is not available on all plans. Navigate to Admin Center People Customers to check for enhanced export options; a basic user export indicates you do not have the add-on.

    What are the export frequency limits? Will my large data export time out? Can I schedule automatic reports?

    Scope and limitations

    UI exports and CSV downloads have no strict frequency limits, but exports exceeding 100,000 records might time out. Split large downloads by date range if needed. API exports have rate limits and require incremental exports for large datasets. You can schedule daily and weekly automatic exports via email for certain data types without affecting manual export usage limits.

    How do I export data from Support? How do I download ticket, user, or organization data?

    Scope and limitations

    You can export ticket, user, or organization data directly from Support or your help desk system. The capability to export data to a JSON, CSV, or XML file is available to admins and agents with a custom role that has export permissions. Verify your pricing tier requirements if the option is missing, or contact Zendesk Customer Support.

    How do I export reports from Explore analytics? Can I download a dashboard?

    Scope and limitations

    To export a dashboard tab from Explore or your reporting tool, navigate to the specific tab. You can only export one tab at a time. To download an individual report, utilize the options within that specific report.

    How do I export data from Sales? How do I download sales data?

    Scope and limitations

    To export data from Sales, utilize the specific export functions within the Zendesk Sales product interface.

articles.name: What is the difference between the solved tickets and tickets solved metrics?
articles.title: What is the difference between the solved tickets and tickets solved metrics?
articles.body:
Question

When creating reports, I noticed that there are multiple different metrics about tickets that are solved. What is the difference between Solved tickets and Tickets solved? 

Answer

Solved tickets

  • This is the number of tickets that are currently in a solved status in your account.
  • Solved tickets appear in the Tickets dataset.
  • If you add the Assignee name attribute under, you will see tickets that are in a solved status. The list will be organized by agent those tickets are currently assigned to. But, as soon as one of those tickets switches back to pending or open, the ticket will no longer show in the report.
    • If you have tickets with custom ticket statuses within the Solved ticket status category, these tickets are also included in the Solved tickets metric.

Tickets solved

  • These are tickets that changed from a different status to a solved status, or a closed status at any point. 
  • This metric excludes tickets moving from a solved status to a closed status so as not to double count tickets.
  • Tickets solved appear in both the Tickets dataset and the Updates history dataset. 

Choose the dataset:

  • In the Updates history dataset, the data depends on how you use it. For example, with the Ticket group attribute, you will see how many tickets moved to a solved or closed status by each agent groups, regardless of what status those tickets are in now.
  • In the Tickets dataset, each metrics specify the timeframe themselves. For example, Tickets solved - last 7 days. To see a list of the tickets solved metrics in the tickets dataset, visit this anchor link and scroll up: Ticket dataset.

Generally, when using a time axis, such as Ticket solved - Date, use the Tickets dataset with the Solved tickets metric. This shows you the tickets that were solved on the given day. There is no need to switch over to the updates history dataset to try and get day-by-day information on what tickets were moved to a solved status.

To see how many tickets were in a solved status on any given day, use the Backlog dataset. Learn more about that unique dataset: Analyzing your ticket backlog history with Explore.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE. ### Concept

What is the Solved tickets metric? What does the Solved tickets metric track in reporting or analytics?

This metric tracks the number of tickets that currently sit in a solved status or state in your account. This includes tickets with custom ticket statuses within the Solved ticket status category.

What is the Tickets solved metric? What does the Tickets solved metric track in reporting or analytics?

This metric tracks tickets that changed from a different progress status to a solved status or a closed status at any point. It excludes tickets moving from a solved status to a closed status so as not to double count tickets.

### Confirmation

Do custom ticket statuses count toward the Solved tickets metric? Do custom states appear in Solved tickets?

Yes. If you have tickets with custom ticket statuses within the Solved ticket status category, the Solved tickets metric includes these tickets.

Pro tip

How do I see tickets solved on a specific day? What is the best practice to report on daily solved tickets?

When you use a time axis like Ticket solved - Date, use the Tickets dataset with the Solved tickets metric. This shows you the tickets that agents solved on the given day, and you do not need to switch over to the updates history dataset to get day-by-day information.

How do I see historical backlog data for solved tickets? How do I view how many tickets were solved on any given day?

To see how many tickets were in a solved status on any given day, use the Backlog dataset.

articles.name: Why doesn't my trigger or automation work on follow-up tickets?
articles.title: Why doesn't my trigger or automation work on follow-up tickets?
articles.body:
Question

I created a business rule for tickets created from the Email channel. Why doesn't it work for follow-up tickets?

Answer

The Email channel condition doesn't apply to follow-up tickets, because these tickets originate from tickets with a Closed status. The correct channel for follow-up tickets is Closed ticket.

If you want your trigger or automation to fire on follow-up tickets in addition to email tickets, add the below conditions under: Meet ANY of the following conditions:

  • Object: Ticket > Channel | Is | Email
  • Object: Ticket > Channel | Is | Closed ticket

For more information, see: About Zendesk channels.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why does a business rule for tickets from the Email channel not work for follow-up tickets? Why do email auto-reply triggers fail on follow-ups? Why does an automation miss follow-up tickets?

The Email channel condition does not apply to follow-up tickets because these tickets originate from tickets with a Closed Ticket Status or state.

What is the correct channel for follow-up tickets? How do you target follow-up tickets in business rules? What channel matches follow-up requests?

The correct channel for follow-up tickets is Closed ticket.

How do I make a trigger or auto-reply rule fire on follow-up tickets? How do I add follow-up tickets to email business rules? How do I set up notification rules for email and follow-up tickets?

Add the below conditions under Meet ANY of the following conditions:

  1. Select Object: Ticket > Channel | Is | Email
  2. Select Object: Ticket > Channel | Is | Closed ticket

articles.name: How do I set up my AI agent to use help center content?
articles.title: How do I set up my AI agent to use help center content?
articles.body:
Question

How do I use an AI agent to generate answers from my help center content? Are the recommended articles based on the organizations that the end users belong to? How can I make sure my articles will appear in AI? How can I create content for AI?

Answer

AI agents respect the end user's access permissions in the help center every time. The AI agent bases AI-created replies and article recommendations on the user segment of the viewer.

Step 1: Create and enable your help center and articles

Before you publish an AI agent, prepare your help center:

  1. Create your help center
  2. Create your categories
  3. Create your sections, within these categories
  4. Create articles, within each section
  5. Ensure your article content follows best practices
  6. Ensure the Visible to field of your article settings shows Everyone, if you want AI to be able to show that information to anyone
  7. Ensure your articles are published
  8. Customize the theme of your help center
  9. Activate your help center   

Step 2: Restrict access to articles for AI with user segments

User segments added to your article settings restrict AI agent access to content

To hide an article from AI agents

  1. Create user segments 
  2. In your article settings, apply that user segments under the Visible to field
  3. You can bulk update articles, too

If your user belongs to 1 user segment but you have multiple user segments

  • The user is in the Premium Customers user segment and is authenticated in messaging
  • The AI agent will surface only articles visible to Everyone and Premium Customers
  • AI responses won't include additional restricted articles, for example, Enterprise Features

If you use no user segments in your article settings

  • Non-authenticated and anonymous end users will be able to access the article from your AI agent
  • The AI agent will only surface visible to everyone, which means public articles without restrictions

The difference between signed-in, authenticated users compared to anonymous users in AI agents

  • For signed-in users, the AI uses their specifically assigned user segments in article settings to show content
  • For anonymous users that are not signed in or authenticated in messaging, the AI assumes no user segment and returns only publicly available help center articles

Step 3: Set up AI agents

To get started with AI agents

  1. Set up messaging, where your AI bot will live
  2. Use Getting started with AI agents. As of May 2026, Zendesk offers a single, unified AI agent experience. Advanced agentic capabilities are now unlocked across all Zendesk Suite and Support plans: 
  3. Use the guided, self-service setup flow to configure your AI agent for simpler use cases across email and messaging
  4. Create a use case or instruction that directs the AI agent to use help center content when it answers customer questions
  5. If needed, configure advanced capabilities, such as agentic logic, multi-step procedures, and external API integrations for more complex tasks
  6. Publish the AI agent
Note for existing customers: If you still use AI agents - Essential or legacy functionality that includes bot builder, Zendesk will stop technical development for these tools by August 31, 2026. End-of-life and full service shut-off will occur on December 10, 2026. Complete your migration to the new experience by August 31 to avoid disruptions.

Understand AI agent terminology

What is the difference between Instructions and Use cases?
  • Instructions are general company policies that apply to everything
  • Use cases are department-specific procedures that apply only to specific situations
Instructions refer to
  • System-level prompts that define the AI agent's overall behavior
  • Examples include "Be friendly and professional", "Offer to escalate to a human agent every time the customer is frustrated", and "Use simple language, and avoid jargon"
  • Sets the overall tone and personality
  • Applies to all conversations
Use cases refer to
  • Specific scenarios that the AI agent handles
  • Examples include Password reset requests, billing questions, and product feature explanations
  • Each use case can have unique logic to address issues
  • The AI routes to the appropriate use case based on specific questions from customers
Deflection
  • The AI agent fully resolves a customer question without human agent involvement
  • Successful deflection means a ticket is prevented
  • Deflection rate is the metric for the percentage of conversations that the AI resolves
Escalation
  • The AI agent transfers the conversation to a human agent
  • This happens when the AI can't answer, the customer explicitly requests a human, or the system detects a complex issue
  • Escalation rate is the metric for the percentage of transferred conversations
Handoff
  • The exact moment a conversation transfers from the AI to a human agent
  • This includes full conversation history so the human agent has immediate context
  • A customer request, an AI decision, or a session timeout can trigger this action
Knowledge base
  • This is the collection of your help center articles
  • The AI agent searches this knowledge base to find and construct answers
  • A more complete knowledge base results in better AI performance

Step 4: Test user segment behavior

To verify the AI agent respects user segments:

  1. Create a test user account assigned to a specific user segment
  2. Sign in as that test user
  3. Ask the AI agent a question about restricted content
  4. Verify the following if AI suggests articles the user can see. The AI shouldn't suggest articles the user can't see

  5. A common issue occurs when the AI agent suggests a restricted article to the wrong user. This occurs because the article's user segment restriction isn't configured correctly. To fix this issue, review the Visible to settings for articles in Knowledge

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    How do I set up my AI agent or chatbot to use help center content or knowledge base help articles? How do I use an AI agent or answer bot to generate answers from my help center content? How can I make sure my articles will appear in AI? How can I create content for AI?

    To configure your AI agent or chatbot to use help articles, follow these steps:

    1. Create your help center or knowledge base.
    2. Create your categories.
    3. Create your sections within these categories.
    4. Create articles within each section.
    5. Ensure your article content follows best practices.
    6. Ensure the Visible to field of your article settings shows Everyone if you want AI to show that information to any visitor or customer.
    7. Ensure your articles are published.
    8. Customize the theme of your help center.
    9. Activate your help center.
    10. Use the guided, self-service setup flow to configure your AI agent across email and messaging.
    11. Create a use case or instruction that directs the AI agent to use help center content when it answers customer questions.
    12. Configure advanced capabilities like agentic logic, multi-step procedures, and external API integrations if needed.
    13. Publish the AI agent.

     

    Are the recommended articles based on the organizations or companies that the end users belong to? Does the AI agent use customer accounts to recommend help articles?

    No. The AI agent or chatbot bases AI-created replies and article recommendations on the user segment of the viewer. AI agents respect the end user's access permissions in the help center or knowledge base every time.

    How do I restrict access to articles for AI with user segments? How do I hide an article from AI agents or chatbots?

    To hide an article from AI agents, restrict access using these steps:

    1. Create user segments.
    2. Apply that user segment under the Visible to field in your article settings.
    You can also bulk update articles to apply these restrictions.

     

    What is the difference between signed-in authenticated users compared to anonymous users or visitors in AI agents? How does the chatbot handle permissions for logged-in clients versus anonymous visitors?

    For signed-in users, the AI uses their specifically assigned user segments in article settings to show content. For example, if an authenticated messaging user belongs to the Premium Customers user segment, the AI agent surfaces only articles visible to Everyone and Premium Customers. For anonymous users or visitors who are not signed in or authenticated in messaging, the AI assumes no user segment and returns only publicly available help center articles.

    What plans support advanced agentic capabilities? Is the unified AI agent experience available on my suite version or pricing tier?

    Zendesk offers a single, unified AI agent experience. Advanced agentic capabilities are unlocked across all Zendesk Suite and Support plans or ticketing systems.

    What is the difference between Instructions and Use cases? What are instructions and use cases for chatbots?

    Instructions are general company policies that define the AI agent's overall behavior, tone, and personality across all conversations. Use cases are department-specific procedures that apply to specific scenarios, such as password reset requests, billing questions, or product feature explanations. The AI routes to the appropriate use case based on the customer's specific question, and each use case can have unique logic.

    How do I test user segment behavior for my AI agent? How do I verify the chatbot respects permissions and access levels?

    To verify the AI agent or answer bot respects user segments, follow these steps:

    1. Create a test user account or customer account assigned to a specific user segment.
    2. Sign in as that test user.
    3. Ask the AI agent a question about restricted content.
    4. Verify if the AI suggests articles the user can see. The AI must not suggest articles the user cannot see.

     

    Why does the AI agent or chatbot suggest a restricted article to the wrong user or customer? How do I fix the chatbot showing restricted help articles to the wrong audience?

    This issue occurs because the article's user segment restriction is incorrect. To fix this issue, review the Visible to settings for articles in Knowledge.

    When is the end-of-life and full service shut-off for AI agents - Essential or legacy functionality? When will Zendesk stop technical development for the legacy bot builder?

    Zendesk stops technical development for AI agents - Essential and legacy functionality including bot builder by August 31, 2026. The official end-of-life and full service shut-off occurs on December 10, 2026. Complete your migration to the new experience by August 31, 2026 to avoid disruptions.

When I edit or save a review, I get an error: Something went wrong (500) or Invalid assignment review request: invalid conversation for assignment.

  2. If possible, submit a review for a different conversation to see if the error is specific to one review.
  3. Follow the steps in: Using Zendesk QA as a reviewer.
  4. If you change a QA category after you submit the review, you can't edit that review. In that case, delete the review and recreate it.
  5. If this message appears: Max message size limit exceeded, divide the information into two sections.

  6. If the issue persists, click: Copy helpful information. I Contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help.

If the issue persists, click Copy helpful information and contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help.

articles.name: Service Incident - June 29, 2026 - AI Agents | Pod 13 - AI Agents bot creating tickets in different account than linked
articles.title: Service Incident - June 29, 2026 - AI Agents | Pod 13 - AI Agents bot creating tickets in different account than linked
articles.body:
Summary

Between June 25, 2026, 00:00 UTC and 22:46 UTC, and again on July 2, 2026, from 20:01 UTC to 22:42 UTC, AI Agents customers in Pod 13 experienced issues where conversations meant for one account were incorrectly routed to another, causing delays and wrong ticket creation. The recurrence on July 2 was resolved by updating the connection settings.

Timeline

June 26, 2026 10:06 UTC | June 26, 2026 3:06 AM PT

We are pleased to inform you that the issue causing AI Agents Advanced conversations to not be responded to within the same ticket has been resolved. We sincerely appreciate your patience as our team diligently addressed this matter.

Root Cause Analysis

The incident was caused by a configuration error where a record was linked to the wrong organization, leading to messages and tickets being routed to the incorrect account.

Resolution

To resolve the issue, we removed outdated webhook information, regenerated the webhook configuration to link correctly to the proper account, and reconnected and fully tested the affected bots to ensure conversations and ticket creation were functioning properly for the correct customers.

Remediation Items

  1. Require formal change requests and peer approval for all production database modifications to ensure careful review and oversight.
  2. Correct the bot configurations related to INC-6427 to prevent recurrence of the issue.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

articles.name: Why is there a 5-second wait time between my voice greetings?
articles.title: Why is there a 5-second wait time between my voice greetings?
articles.body:
Question

Why is there a 5-second wait time between my voice greetings?

Answer

A 5-second delay is expected if one of the following functions is enabled in voice settings:

  • IVR
  • Callback
  • Call recording consent
  • Voicemail

If you'd like to avoid a 5-second wait time after the Voicemail and Callback greetings, you can contact Zendesk Customer Support and ask to disable the delay for your account.

articles.name: Workflow: How can I use OAuth to authenticate Zendesk API calls in custom actions?
articles.title: Workflow: How can I use OAuth to authenticate Zendesk API calls in custom actions?
articles.body:
To authenticate Zendesk API calls in custom actions you must create a connection, such as what is described in Creating connections to integrate with external services. 

This workflow includes the steps to achieve this below: 

  • Step 1: Create the Zendesk OAuth client
  • Step 2: Add Zendesk client as a Connections client
  • Step 3: Create the Connection
  • Step 4: Use the Connection in a Custom Action

Step 1: Create the Zendesk OAuth client

  • In Admin Center, click  Apps and integrations in the sidebar, and select APIs > OAuth clients 
  • Click Add OAuth client on the right side of the OAuth client list
  • Set the Client kind to Confidential
  • Set the redirect URL to https://zis.zendesk.com/api/services/zis/connections/oauth/callback (note that the subdomain is always zis, not your account subdomain)
  • Click Save. After the page refreshes, a new pre-populated Secret field appears on the lower side. This is the client_secret value specified in the OAuth 2.0 spec.
  • Copy the Secret value to your clipboard and save it somewhere safe. Note that the characters may extend past the width of the text box, so ensure to select everything before copying. For security reasons, your secret displays fully only once. After clicking Save, you only have access to the first nine characters.
  • For a full description of the fields mentioned in this step, see Using OAuth authentication with your application

Step 2: Add Zendesk client as a Connections client

  • In Admin Center, click  Apps and integrations in the sidebar, and select Connections > OAuth Clients
  • Click Add client on the right side of the Connections OAuth client list
  • Set the OAuth grant type to Authorization code
  • Add the client ID and secret from your Zendesk client created in Step 1
  • Set the Authorize URL to: https://{your_subdomain}.zendesk.com/oauth/authorizations/new
  • Set the Token URL to: https://{your_subdomain}.zendesk.com/oauth/tokens
  • Enter a space-separate list of default scopes. Scopes are permissions that represent what a client application can access on behalf of the user. Note that it is recommended to only set the minimum necessary scopes for your end goal (avoid using the blanket read/write unless necessary). The available scopes are documented here.
  • Click Save to create the client

Step 3: Create the Connection

  • In Admin Center, click  Apps and integrations in the sidebar, and select Connections > OAuth Clients
  • Click Create connection
  • Set the Authentication type to OAuth2.0
  • Set the scopes you want for this specific connection, or leave blank to inherit the default scopes from the client
  • Set the Allowed Domain to your Zendesk account URL {your_subdomain}.zendesk.com

  • After you click save, you go through the popup OAuth authorization screen. Click Allow.

Step 4: Use the Connection in a Custom Action

  • At this point, the connection you created in step 3 is available in the Authentication dropdown when you create a custom action
  • Tip: Keep in mind that if you edit an existing custom action to include this authorization, you must re-add it to any action flows where it is used. Changes to custom actions do not automatically reflect in any action flows that include it.

articles.name: Service Incident - July 1, 2026 - Sunshine Conversations | Multiple Pods - Malformed AI Agent “ask for details” forms via social channels
articles.title: Service Incident - July 1, 2026 - Sunshine Conversations | Multiple Pods - Malformed AI Agent “ask for details” forms via social channels
articles.body:
Summary

On June 25, 2026 from 14:59 UTC to July 1, 2026 13:46 UTC, customers using social messaging channels (such as WhatsApp) experienced an issue where the “ask for details” form displayed broken or missing labels, which made it difficult (and in some cases impossible) for end-users to complete and submit support requests.

Timeline

July 01, 2026 09:17 UTC | July 01, 2026 2:17 AM PT 

We’re actively investigating an issue impacting customer's end-users using the AI Agent “ask for details” forms via social channels app such as WhatsApp. We’re working on a fix and will share another update as soon as we have more information.

July 01, 2026 12:29 UTC | July 01, 2026 5:29 AM PT 

Our Engineering team is currently deploying a fix for this issue. We’ll update you once the deployment is complete. Thank you for your patience as we work to resolve it.

July 01, 2026 13:51 UTC | July 01, 2026 6:51 AM PT 

Our engineers have released and tested a fix for the issue impacting AI Agent "ask for details" forms. The issue is now resolved and all forms should behave as expected. Thank you for your patience while we worked to resolve this incident.

Root Cause Analysis

This incident was caused by a gap in our release process that allowed an update to be published without all of the required language resources for the “ask for details” form. Because those label files were missing, the form could not display its text correctly and appeared broken for customers using social messaging channels.

Resolution

To fix this issue, we built and deployed an updated version of the affected component that correctly included the missing language/label resources. Once the updated version finished deploying across our environments, the form labels returned to normal and customers confirmed the experience was working again.

Remediation Items

  1. Add monitoring and alerts for missing language files so issues like broken or missing labels are detected quickly.
  2. Create a documented recovery process for restoring missing language files so we can fix this kind of problem faster and more consistently.
  3. Add automated checks to detect out-of-date translations to reduce the chance of incomplete or stale text appearing in customer-facing forms.
  4. Improve form layout resilience when text is wrong or unusually long so buttons and key actions stay visible and usable even if labels render incorrectly.
  5. Update the release pipeline to fail the build when required language files are missing so incomplete releases can’t be published

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Release notes through 2026-07-03
articles.title: Release notes through 2026-07-03
articles.body:
See the What's New section for an overview of what was released last month.

This week's release notes include:

  • Voice
  • Knowledge
  • AI agents - Advanced
  • Analytics
  • App Marketplace
  • Products with no updates this week

Voice

New:

  • Admins can now restrict outbound calling to specific groups of agents. See Managing phone line settings.

Fixed:

  • Fixed a bug in which calls were reported as assigned to the default group even when the line was external. In fact, external numbers cannot receive calls, so the group should be "NULL". This affected only omnichannel contact center customers.

Knowledge

New:

  • The Zendesk connector can now ingest articles from private help centers
  • The Zendesk connector supports PDFs as article attachments. To get a feature, enroll in the EAP.
  • Box and S3 support PDFs as files. To get a feature, enroll in the EAP.
  • The Knowledge copilot recommendations section now includes a "View all recommendations" link that takes you straight to your full list of article and procedure recommendations in Admin Center.

AI agents - Advanced

New:

  • Zendesk schedules are now supported in AI agents, allowing the exclusion of holidays from regular working hours when checking agent availability. See Checking business hours and agent availability during AI agent conversations and Configuring escalation strategies and flows for AI agents.

Analytics

New:

  • Customer satisfaction (CSAT) ratings are now available in the real-time monitoring ticket progress dashboard. This enhancement allows supervisors and team leads to monitor customer satisfaction performance alongside ticket workflows, enabling faster responses to customer sentiment and more informed team management decisions.

App Marketplace

New:
  • Clonepartner (Support)
    • Clonepartner delivers a fully customizable migration tailored to your exact data structures. Migrate your tickets, contacts, and custom fields from any help desk with zero downtime. Run precise point-in-time migrations for the delta to ensure a seamless transition. Connect your account securely via OAuth and Clonepartner will handle the heavy lifting.
  • PII Shield (Support) (paid)
    • PII Shield scans Zendesk tickets for sensitive personal information and gives agents the tools to permanently remove it — before it's shared, stored, or audited. All detection runs in the agent's browser. Ticket content is never sent to external servers, never processed by third-party AI, and never stored outside Zendesk. This is a genuine compliance advantage for regulated industries. After every redaction, PII Shield posts a structured internal note to the ticket recording what was removed, which agent removed it, and the exact timestamp. This note is your durable audit trail.
  • Netgsm SMS (Support)
    • Netgsm SMS allows your support agents to send SMS messages directly from Zendesk tickets and user profiles without leaving the support interface. Send SMS to customers directly from any ticket or user sidebar. Select from your registered sender names and view SMS delivery status in real time.
  • WitWave by Witbor (Support)
    • WitWave by Witbor closes the gap in messaging conversations: your agents can listen, visualize, and send WhatsApp voice notes directly from the ticket interface, without opening external apps and without losing context. Play back previous voice notes of the conversation with an interactive waveform, directly from the ticket. Record new voice notes from the ticket panel and send them to the customer in a single click. WitWave detects whether the ticket is on the WhatsApp channel and alerts the agent before recording, preventing send errors.
  • KnowledgeBridge (Support)
    • KnowledgeBridge automatically generates llms.txt and llms-full.txt files from your Zendesk Help Center, making your knowledge base immediately accessible to AI systems, LLM-powered agents, and automated tooling. With one click, KnowledgeBridge fetches all published articles from your Help Center, converts them to clean markdown, and assembles two machine-readable output files, llms.txt and llms-full.txt

Products with no updates this week

  • Admin Center
  • Copilot
  • AI agents
  • Support
  • Zendesk Workforce Management (WFM)
  • Zendesk QA

articles.name: Service Incident - June 26, 2026 - Sell | All Pods - Deals feature outage in Zendesk Sales and SQL errors due to unintentional dropped DB tables
articles.title: Service Incident - June 26, 2026 - Sell | All Pods - Deals feature outage in Zendesk Sales and SQL errors due to unintentional dropped DB tables
articles.body:
Summary

Between June 26, 2026, 17:37 UTC and June 28, 2026, 03:45 UTC, Zendesk Sales customers across multiple pods experienced an outage and degradation of the Deals feature. Issues included problems loading, saving, or creating Deals, blank pages, and missing data, occurring while database restoration was underway.

Timeline

June 26, 2026 19:03 UTC | June 26, 2026 12:03 PM PT

We are investigating an issue preventing some Zendesk Sales customers from accessing features within the Sales product, including Deals. We will provide an update as soon as new information is available.

June 26, 2026 19:53 UTC | June 26, 2026 12:53 PM PT

Our engineers are continuing to work towards restoring access to all Sales product features. We appreciate your patience and understanding. We will provide an update by the next hour or sooner if we have new information to share.

June 26, 2026 22:54 UTC | June 26, 2026 3:54 PM PT

Our engineers continue to work to restore the impacted Sales product features, with a focus on the most impactful.  We appreciate your patience while we work on this, we will provide an update within the next 3 hours or sooner if we have new information to share.

June 26, 2026 23:23 UTC | June 26, 2026 4:23 PM PT

The ability to save new deals has been restored and users should be able to operate as expected. Our engineers will continue to work towards restoring any lost data during the outage.  Please let us know ASAP if you are still seeing impact. Thank you for your patience as we resolve this issue.

June 27, 2026 3:50 UTC | June 26, 2026 8:50 PM PT

Our engineering team is actively working to restore all data impacted by the outage, including custom field data. We sincerely appreciate your patience and understanding as we work to fully stabilize the system. We will keep you informed of any new developments as they become available.

June 27, 2026 5:27 UTC | June 26, 2026 10:27 PM PT

Our Engineering team is actively working to restore the data affected, including custom field data. Please check your system and let us know if you continue to experience issues viewing the custom field data. We appreciate your patience as our team works diligently to fully resolve this matter.

June 28, 2026 5:05 UTC | June 27, 2026 10:05 PM PT

Thank you for your patience while we worked towards resolution. We have now completed the full remediation of the impacted data, including the custom field data. All functionality has been restored, we will continue to monitor through the weekend. Please let us know if you are still seeing any impact to your systems, data, or functionality, thank you.

July 1, 2026 5:56 UTC | June 30, 2026 10:56 PM PT

We’ve completed the full remediation of the impacted data, and functionality has been fully restored, including the ability to view and save new deals.

Root Cause Analysis

The incident was caused by an accidental command during maintenance that unintentionally deleted seven important tables in the Sales database, resulting in errors and an outage of the Deals feature until the data was restored from backup.

Resolution

To resolve the issue, the seven accidentally deleted Sales database tables were restored from a recent backup. Data was carefully reconciled to prevent conflicts, and missing information was replenished through a controlled recovery process. The team closely monitored system health and customer tickets until full access and functionality were confirmed.

Remediation Items

  1. Review and update user permissions, removing unnecessary "Drop" privileges.
  2. Increase team awareness by defining clear environment-specific access controls.
  3. Review and regulate activities scheduled on Fridays to reduce risk.
  4. Implement safeguards to prevent harmful database commands from being executed in the production environment.
  5. Investigate and resolve the intermittent issue affecting the 'design1st' customer’s deal field updates.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: Upgraded Premier plan features
articles.title: Upgraded Premier plan features
articles.body:
Overview

This article explains the Premier support features—including VIP Case Management, Service Reviews, and HyperCare & Peak Event Management—introduced as part of the recent Premier plan upgrade. It outlines how these dedicated and proactive support features operate, providing clarity on the enhanced technical resources and environment optimization opportunities now available to help customers get the most from their new plan benefits.

The following features are discussed in this documentation:

  • VIP Case Management
  • Service Reviews
  • HyperCare and Peak Event Management

What is VIP Case Management?

VIP Case Management is a dedicated support model included in Premier Plus and Premier Enterprise plans. Instead of working with a rotating pool of support agents, you are assigned a designated Premier Support Engineer who serves as your single point of contact for all technical support on your account.

Your Premier Support Engineer takes ownership of all open support tickets, ensures each one is actively progressing toward resolution, and coordinates across Zendesk teams on your behalf when needed.

What are Service Reviews?

A Service Review is a structured meeting between you and your Premier Support Engineer covering key metrics and trends from your Zendesk environment. The goal is to identify patterns, surface optimization opportunities, and keep your support operation running effectively. The service review provides you with an opportunity to meet directly with our PSE and partner together to be prepared for any upcoming changes that may require support level intervention.

What topics are covered in a Service Review?

Each Service Review covers the following areas:

  • Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) scores
  • Product area breakdown
  • Resolution type analysis
  • First Reply Time
  • Requester Wait Time
  • Customer usage trends and service patterns
  • Optimization opportunities

How often do Service Reviews take place and who runs them? 

Your assigned Premier Support Engineer leads every Service Review, which takes place twice a year on Premier Plus and monthly on Premier Enterprise.

What is HyperCare and Peak Event Management?

HyperCare is a proactive support process designed to prepare your Zendesk environment for high-volume events such as product launches, sales periods, or other planned spikes in customer demand.

Note: HyperCare and Peak Event Management are available on Premier Enterprise only.

It is a structured, proactive process owned by your Premier Support Engineer that ensures Zendesk is prepared to support your business during periods of expected high volume. Rather than reacting to problems as they arise, your Premier Support Engineer works with you and internal Zendesk teams ahead of the event.

The HyperCare process includes the following:

  • An event management playbook documenting your event specifics, timelines, and expected volume
  • Advance notification to all relevant internal Zendesk teams before your event begins
  • A proactive monitoring ticket created by your Premier Support Engineer with a ticket classification of "Major," enabling near-immediate 24/7 support response if issues arise during the event
  • Proactive performance monitoring and automated alerts throughout the event window where applicable

What is an event management playbook?

It is an internal document created by your Premier Support Engineer that captures the details of your upcoming event, including expected volume increases, key dates, and any specific concerns or dependencies. This playbook is shared internally at Zendesk to align all relevant teams before the event begins.

What kind of response time can I expect during a HyperCare event?

During an active HyperCare event, the proactive monitoring ticket is classified as "Major," which enables an almost immediate 24/7 response from Zendesk support if issues occur.

If you have any questions, or would like to find out more about how to upgrade to the new plans, please contact your Zendesk Account Team or Customer Success Manager.

Additional resources
